name: Arizona Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Department of Health Services — Speech-Language Pathology

address.description: Arizona Department of Health Services, Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology & Audiology, 150 North 18th Avenue, Suite 410, Phoenix, AZ 85007

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

requiredDocuments.name: Fingerprint clearance card
requiredDocuments.description: Arizona DPS fingerprint clearance card required.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Arizona SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is approximately $150. Biennial renewal is $100.

mainEntity.name: Is Arizona part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Arizona has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

mainEntity.name: Does Arizona allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Arizona have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Arizona. To work in Arizona, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Arizona?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
